warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
The fukui-reactivity task on nitrobenzene was performed using DFT at the tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fit) in PySCF, gas phase, with charge 0 and multiplicity 1. The input geometry was provided from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz`; this was a single-point calculation on that fixed geometry. The partial charges used for calculating Fukui indices were Mulliken charges from PySCF.

The most electrophilic site was found to be the Carbon atom at atom label 4 (0-based index 3) with a Fukui f+ index of 0.49641782. The most nucleophilic site was found to be the Carbon atom at atom label 5 (0-based index 4) with a Fukui f- index of 0.1072882.

All SCF calculations for the neutral, cation, and anion states converged.
